sections.paragraph:
δικαίως adv. of δίκαιος (Hom.+).

① pert. to being just or right in a juridical sense, justly, in an upright manner

ⓐ of a judge’s hearing of a case (judge) uprightly, fairly (IPriene 63, 20 and oft. in ins; Diod S 15, 11, 2 δ. κρίνειν; Sir 35:18 v.l.) 1 Pt 2:23; B 19:11; D 4:3. Sarcastically GPt 3:7.

ⓑ of treatment in a deserving manner for one’s way of life uprightly, justly, in (all) justice (X., Symp. 4, 60; Lucian, Dial. Mort. 30, 1 δ. κολασθήσομαι; Just., A I, 43, 8 δ. κολάσεως ἐτύγχανεν; Tat. 7:1) πάσχειν (Wsd 19:13; TestSim 4:3; Just., D. 110, 6) Hs 6, 3, 6. λέγειν 1:4. ἀπολέσθαι B 5:4; καὶ ἡμεῖς μὲν δ. and we have been justly condemned Lk 23:41. Opp. οὐ δ. quite unjustly 1 Cl 44:3 (Just., D. 107, 3).

② pert. to quality of character, thought, or behavior, correctly, justly, uprightly (w. ὁσίως, ἀμέμπτως) 1 Th 2:10; (w. ὁσίως) PtK 2 p. 15, 2 (Pla., Rep. 1, 331a ὁσίως κ. δικαίως; IPriene 46, 12 [I b.c.]; 60, 8f [II b.c.]; UPZ 144, 13 [164 b.c.] τοῖς θεοῖς, πρὸς οὓς ὁσίως … καὶ δικαίως πολιτευσάμενος; En 106:18 δ. κ. ὁσίως; Jos., Ant. 6, 87; cp. Ar. 15, 10); ἀναστρέφεσθαι 2 Cl 5:6; (w. σωφρόνως, εὐσεβῶς) ζῆν Tit 2:12 (Heraclides, Pol. 39 δ. κ. σωφρόνως βιοῦσι); cp. 1 Cl 51:2; 62:1. ἀγαπᾶν love rightly IEph 15:3. νοεῖν understand rightly B 10:12. ἐκνήφειν be sober, as you ought 1 Cor 15:34; δ. ἀναμένειν τινά wait for someone in uprightness IMagnMai 9, 3; δ. νοεῖν understand correctly B 10:12.—M-M. s.v. δίκαιος.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

oft. oft. = often

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

TestSim TestSim = Testament of Simeon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Hs Hs = Similitudes

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IMagnMai IMagnMai = Die Inschriften von Magnesia am Mäander—Lists 3

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 250
